title: SEC2: notify_closure leaked on every set_event_callback call
description:
File: wasm/src/client.rs line 250.

wire_notify_callback ends with notify_closure.forget(), which leaks the Closure allocation into the JS GC permanently. The API documentation (api.rs line 151) explicitly states 'Replaces any previously registered callback.' Each replacement call to on_event -> set_event_callback -> wire_notify_callback leaks the previous Closure — it is never freed.

Cost: each call to on_event leaks one Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)> (~heap allocation for the Box<dyn FnMut> vtable + captures including the Rc<RefCell<Vec<UserInfo>>> clone). With repeated callback registration (e.g. React component remounts, hot-reload during development) this accumulates unbounded memory.

Fix: Store the Closure in the struct rather than forgetting it. Change notify_cb field in WasmTransport (or NieRelayClient) from Option<Function> to Option<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>. Replacing it drops the old Closure, which calls set_onmessage(None) or equivalent cleanup automatically. This requires restructuring the callback ownership but eliminates the leak entirely.

Classify: P2 (confirmed memory leak on every callback replacement; compounds with SPA frameworks that remount components)
close_reason: duplicate of nie-7bv6.28: same notify_closure forget() leak; nie-7bv6.28 covers the issue at P1 with full context
